prologue.title: Latest Representation
prologue.narrator: The rain has fallen on Valmire for so long that even the return looks like an arrival.
prologue.text:
The train stops slowly in the rain.

You're going down to the dock alone.

The station seems normal.

Old.

Tired.

But normal.

Some travellers are still waiting under the yellow lamps.

None of them really look at you.

A strange feeling goes through you.

Like you've arrived somewhere where nobody really expected you.

Alone.

Lost.

You're watching the invitation in your hand.

> "The Théâtre de Valmire invites you to the last performance. »

Your name is written on the back.

You don't recognize writing.

In the distance, slow music resonates in the city.

For a second, you feel strange that you've heard this rain before.

voyageurs.title: Travellers
voyageurs.narrator: People sometimes avoid the eyes of those who arrive.
voyageurs.text:
The few travelers present seem nervous.

A woman quickly folds her diary when you approach.

A man constantly looks at the clock.

23:17.

You notice that no one gets on the train that just arrived.

invitation.title: Invitation
invitation.narrator: Some invitations are more like convocations.
invitation.text:
The paper is old.

Very old.

The ink seems more recent.

On the back of the invitation, under your name, a half erased sentence appears:

> "Don't miss the last act. »

train.title: The Train
train.narrator: It is sometimes harder to leave than to arrive.
train.text:
You're heading towards the train.

The doors are closed.

The driver's gone.

Lights are slowly extinguishing car after car.

Then the train goes back into the night.

Empty.

rue_principale.title: The Rues de Valmire
rue_principale.narrator: Valmire seems suspended between two epochs.
rue_principale.text:
The streets are quiet.

Too quiet.

Yellow posters cover the walls.

All announce the same thing:

> LAST REPRESENTATION

The inhabitants seem polite.

But tired.

Like they haven't slept for a long time.

choices.text: Follow the music to the theatre
Avoid theatre and walk at random

cafe.title: The Café des Voyageurs
cafe.narrator: Some cities hide their secrets behind music.
cafe.text:
The coffee is warm.

A pianist plays a slow melody.

Clients speak in a low voice.

You notice several watches stopped at the same time.

choices.text: Stay at the cafe and ignore the theater

pianiste.title: The Pianist
pianiste.narrator: Artists sometimes notice what others don't know.
pianiste.text:
The pianist continues to play without raising his eyes.

> "This city loves sad stories. »

Then:

> "People come here to forget certain things. »

He stops for a second.

> "Or to remember it. »

hotel.title: Hotel Valmire
hotel.text:
The receptionist smiled at you immediately.

Like he's waiting for you.

> "Are you staying for the performance? »

choices.text: Why is everyone talking about the theater?

hotel_suite.title: The Chamber
hotel_suite.narrator: The nights in Valmire seem longer than elsewhere.
hotel_suite.text:
Your room overlooks the theater.

In the night:
- you hear applause,
- steps in the hallway,
- then silence.

When you open the door:

The hallway is empty.

choices.text: Watch the theater from the window

clinique.title: The Abandoned Clinic
clinique.text:
The clinic seems to have been abandoned for a long time.

Medical records are still in the offices.

All talk:
- insomnia,
- obsessions,
- memory loss,
- and a room called The King in Yellow.

choices.text: Find the name of the theatre

theatre.title: The Theatre of Valmire
theatre.text:
The building is beautiful.

Almost unreal in the rain.

Inside, a receptionist smiles softly.

> « Welcome to Valmire. »

manuscrit.title: The Manuscript
manuscrit.narrator: Some stories change according to who reads them.
manuscrit.text:
The manuscript is ancient.

Very old.

The play tells a city that refuses to die.

A sentence draws your attention:

> "The curtain never really falls. »

coulisses.title: Behind the scenes
coulisses.narrator: Behind each representation is something hidden.
coulisses.text:
The actors repeat in silence.

Their lips are moving.

But no sound comes out.

Behind the scenes:
- a red door,
- a clock stopped,
- a huge painting by Valmire.

tableau.title: The Table
tableau.text:
The painting represents Valmire in the rain.

Something seems different.

You could swear a silhouette wasn't there a few seconds earlier.

A silhouette that looks like you.

porte_intro.title: Red Gate
porte_intro.narrator: Some doors seem older than the surrounding walls.
porte_intro.text:
The red door is locked.

Yet you have the strange impression that it was open before.

Or maybe it's just an impression.

porte_rouge.title: The Second Act
porte_rouge.narrator: The second act is not intended for spectators.
porte_rouge.text:
Behind the door:

An empty room.

Dozens of chairs.

All turned to a black scene.

On some chairs:

Photographs.

Some show you.

At times you don't remember.

scene_finale.title: The Last Act
scene_finale.text:
When you get on stage:

The lights are on.

Seats are now occupied.

But all faces are blurred.

Then the narrator speaks directly.

> "You wanted to understand. »

fin_silence.title: Silence
fin_silence.text:
Fire slowly devours the theater.

The rain finally stops.

Before everything goes away:

You hear applause.

Even without a public.

fin_roi.title: The King in Yellow
fin_roi.text:
The yellow curtain falls slowly.

Then the doors of the theatre open.

A new person enters Valmire.

Lost.

Like you at first.

oubli.title: The Forget
oubli.narrator: We can leave a city. Not always what we saw.
oubli.text:
You're leaving Valmire before dawn.

Several weeks go by.

Then one night:

You find the manuscript at home.

Opened to a page you don't remember reading.

femme_journal.title: The Woman in the Journal
femme_journal.narrator: Some people hide secrets less than memories.
femme_journal.text:
The woman holds her diary against her.

You only see the title before she folds it:

> THE THEASTER ROUNDS THIS SOIR

When you ask her if she knows the play, she answers too quickly:

**"No one really knows this play. We only think we remember. *

choices.text: Read the newspaper until you feel dizzy

horloge.title: The Clock
horloge.narrator: Time is sometimes the first lie in a city.
horloge.text:
The clock says 23:17.

The second needle moves normally.

Yet the time does not change.

A man next to you whispers:

**"She always ends up leaving. *

Then he moves away before you can answer.

choices.text: Remain to fix the clock until the station seems to change

journal.title: The Local Journal
journal.narrator: The newspapers tell what cities agree to admit.
journal.text:
The newspaper talks about an old fire in the theater.

The date is partially erased.

These include:
- missing actors,
- a performance interrupted,
- from a director never found.

No name is legible.

Except for a word, printed at the bottom of the page:

**CARCOSA**

choices.text: Ask the pianist what Carcosa is
Read the article from the beginning

clients.title: Customers
clients.narrator: A shared silence can become heavier than a confession.
clients.text:
Clients don't talk much.

They all have a cup in front of them.

None smoke.

A young man scratches something on a towel.

When he gets up, you read:

> Don't go tonight.

Then the ink spreads under a drop of water falling from the ceiling.

choices.text: Fix the towel until the ink disappears

pianiste_suite.title: Melody
pianiste_suite.narrator: Some music is not in the ears. They stay somewhere else.
pianiste_suite.text:
The pianist smiles without joy.

**"This song is not mine. Nobody here knows who wrote it. *

He plays three notes.

You recognize them.

Impossible.

You're sure you heard them in your dream the night before.

But you haven't slept in Valmire yet.

choices.text: Ask the name of the song
Listen to the melody until you forget where you are

couloir.title: The Corridor
couloir.narrator: Hotels always have too many doors.
couloir.text:
The hallway is empty.

Yet you hear steps behind every door.

In the back, a room is closed.

On the handle hangs a sign:

> Do not disturb.

Your name is written in pencil underneath.

fenetre.title: The Window
fenetre.narrator: From some windows, we see less the world than a possible version of the world.
fenetre.text:
The theater shines in the rain.

A line of spectators slowly enters.

Then you blink.

The place is empty.

The theater doors are closed.

The music continues.

choices.text: Watch the theater until the rain becomes silent

reve.title: The Yellow Dream
reve.narrator: Dreams are sometimes the general repetitions of things that have not yet taken place.
reve.text:
You dream of an empty scene.

A yellow curtain falls slowly.

Someone's cheering.

Only one person.

When you wake up, your hands smell like theater dust.

choices.text: Close your eyes and follow the dream to the end

temoignages.title: Testimonials
temoignages.narrator: Patients often use the same words when no one has spoken to them.
temoignages.text:
The testimonies describe contradictory symptoms.

Some talk about dreams.
Others from a cult.
Others from memory disease.

Only one detail always comes back:

> the music starts before the performance.

A handwritten note adds:

> Maybe the problem isn't the play.
> Maybe it prevents something else from going out.

archives_theatre.title: Archives du Théâtre
archives_theatre.narrator: Administrations sometimes classify inexplicable as ordinary cases.
archives_theatre.text:
You find a dossier dedicated to the Valmire Theatre.

He mentions a fire.

But the dates don't match.

1921.
1947.
1973.
2009.

Each document speaks of the same fire.

As if it had happened several times.

choices.text: Back to the streets of Valmire
Compare all dates until they merge

receptionniste.title: Receptionist
receptionniste.narrator: Sadness is sometimes a form of politeness.
receptionniste.text:
The receptionist thought before answering.

**"No one really knows who wrote the play. *

Then she adds:

**"Some say it was found. Others she wrote herself. I think she was just waiting for her audience. *

choices.text: Ask why this is the last representation

representation.title: Representation
representation.narrator: The curtain always rises too slowly.
representation.text:
The room is almost full.

Yet nobody talks.

On stage, actors start a common scene:

A traveller arrives in a city in the rain.

You smile nervously.

Then the lead actor pronounces your name.

choices.text: Follow the actors after the stage
Do nothing and look to the end

lecture_suite.title: The Next Page
lecture_suite.narrator: You never read the same story twice.
lecture_suite.text:
The following page describes a city in the rain.

A train station.
Coffee.
A hotel.
A clinic.

Everything seems familiar.

But no passage clearly states what happens next.

Only this sentence:

> The spectator will choose the form of his prison.

choices.text: Referring the manuscript and returning to the lobby
Read the first line of the manuscript

ecriture.title: Same Hand
ecriture.narrator: Recognising writing can be more worrying than recognizing a face.
ecriture.text:
You compare the handwriting of the manuscript with that of the invitation.

The letters are similar.

Not identical.

As if the same hand had written both texts years apart.

Or as if someone was imitating a writing he knew too well.

Yours.

choices.text: Keep the invitation and leave the archives

acteur.title: The Muet Actor
acteur.narrator: Actors sometimes know their role without knowing the play.
acteur.text:
The actor is watching you.

He opens his mouth.

No sound comes out.

Then he quickly wrote on his sleeve with black makeup:

> Do not end the scene.

Before you can react, he erases the message.

tableau_suite.title: The Silhouette
tableau_suite.narrator: An image shouldn't wait till we look at it.
tableau_suite.text:
The silhouette in the painting moves one step forward.

The painted street looks deeper than before.

At the bottom, you distinguish the station.

Then the coffee.

Then you, in front of the painting.

The painting now represents the room where you stand.

reception_porte.title: The Forbidden Question
reception_porte.narrator: Some questions close faces faster than doors.
reception_porte.text:
When you mention the red door, the receptionist stops smiling.

**" It is only open when someone insists long enough. *

She then added, almost to regret:

**"Most people confuse response with deliverance. *

choices.text: Ask why she refuses to talk about it

photos.title: Photographs
photos.narrator: Evidence doesn't explain anything. It only makes doubt more precise.
photos.text:
The photographs show you in Valmire.

In front of the station.
Coffee.
In the hotel.
Behind the scenes.

On one of them, you wear different clothes.

On another, you're much older.

On the back of the last photo:

> Again.

spectateur.title: The Spectator
spectateur.narrator: Stories die only when nobody looks at them anymore.
spectateur.text:
You're watching the audience.

Faces get sharp.

One by one.

All yours.

Different versions.
Different ages.
Different choices.

So you understand one thing without being able to formulate it:

Valmire wasn't expecting an actor.

She was waiting for someone to leave the room before the end.

The theater becomes silent.

Very slowly, the lights go out.

Then you notice something.

Over the hall.

A balcony.

You don't remember going there.

Yet you are there now.

Below you:

- the scene is empty,
- the seats too,
- as if the performance had just ended.

Then, far away...

A train.

The metal noise resonates at night.

You're approaching the balcony.

Through the rain, you see Valmire station.

The train just arrived.

A silhouette slowly descends from the car.

Alone.

Lost.

For a second...

She seems familiar.

Then the lights of the theatre are completely extinguished.

And the music starts again.

retour_gare.title: Back to the station
retour_gare.narrator: Some paths don't go back. They're taking the same place.
retour_gare.text:
You're blinking.

The music goes out.

Rain's coming back.

When you open your eyes, you're back on the dock.

The train just arrived.

In your hand:

the yellow invitation.

It's dry.

Unlike you.

acteur_question.title: The Muette Scene
acteur_question.narrator: The silent answers are sometimes the clearest.
acteur_question.text:
The actor trembles slightly.

He looks towards the stage, then towards the red door at the bottom of the corridor.

He's not moving.

He only writes a word on his sleeve:

> Later.

Then he regains his place among the other actors, as if nothing had happened.

choices.text: Follow the actors despite the malaise

tableau_contact.title: Cold Painting
tableau_contact.narrator: An image should not have a temperature.
tableau_contact.text:
Your fingers touch the paint.

It's cold.

Not like a canvas.

Like a window overlooking a rain night.

For a second, you hear the station.

Then everything becomes quiet again.

The figure in the painting has disappeared.

choices.text: Let the vertigo take you back to the station

reception_secret.title: What she doesn't say
reception_secret.narrator: Some secrets are not kept out of loyalty, but out of fear of what they would do once released.
reception_secret.text:
The receptionist shakes her hands in front of her.

**"I do not refuse to talk about it. I refuse to send you there. *

She looks at the doors of the main hall.

**"Start with the play. Understand the actors. Listen to the music. After only... You will know if this door deserves to be opened. *

porte_verrouillee.title: Locked
porte_verrouillee.narrator: Not all important doors open to the first act.
porte_verrouillee.text:
You shoot the handle.

The door won't move.

A copper plate is attached to it.

> Access reserved after the performance.

When you raise your eyes, the plaque disappeared.

Or maybe you didn't see her well.

apres_representation.title: After the Intermission
apres_representation.narrator: It is often between two acts that real things move.
apres_representation.text:
The actors leave the scene in silence.

Nobody seems to be breathing.

At the bottom of the corridor, the red door is now closed.

This time, nobody's keeping you close.

choices.text: Follow the actors in the lodges
The door seems to be waiting for you. But something's holding you back.

fin_dernier_train.title: The Last Train
fin_dernier_train.text:
You refuse Valmire before you even enter.

You go back to the dock and wait for the next train.

He's finally coming.

Empty.

The doors open without noise.

You're going up.

The train leaves at night.

The hours pass.

No station appears.

Through the window, in the rain, you see a yellow poster stuck inside the car.

> LAST REPRESENTATION

Your reflection smiles before you.

fin_oubli_heureux.title: The Happy Bliss
fin_oubli_heureux.narrator: Not all prisons look like cages.
fin_oubli_heureux.text:
You decide not to go to the theater.

The coffee is hot.

The music is soft.

The locals are gradually ceasing to seem strange to you.

You have a drink.

Then another.

You laugh with people you don't know their name.

For several years, you never think of Valmire again.

Then, one night, in a city you don't recognize, someone plays three piano notes.

And it all comes back.

fin_patient.title: The Patient
fin_patient.narrator: Reason can also become a form of surrender.
fin_patient.text:
You stay at the clinic.

You read the files over and over again.

Everything becomes clear.

Theatre is only a symbol.

Music, trauma.

Posters, hallucinations.

You accept the treatment found in the old firm.

When you open your eyes, you're tied to a white bed.

A voice behind the door whispers:

**"The patient always refuses representation. *

In the distance, you hear applause.

fin_acteur.title: The Actor
fin_acteur.narrator: By following the roles of others, we end up receiving his.
fin_acteur.text:
You stay with the actors.

You observe their actions.

Their silence.

Their way of breathing all at the same pace.

Someone's giving you a suit.

You want to refuse.

But your hands accept it before you.

We make up your face.

We'll put you in front of the curtain.

When the light lights up, you already know your text.

You open your mouth.

No sound comes out.

The audience applauds.

fin_ville_vide.title: The Empty City
fin_ville_vide.narrator: A place can survive its inhabitants. Not always to his story.
fin_ville_vide.text:
You don't want the theater.

You're walking in Valmire for hours.

Coffee closes.

The hotel's gone.

The silhouettes disappear from the windows.

Even the rain ends.

The city becomes perfectly silent.

Then you understand.

There was nobody watching you.

There was only the city itself.

In the distance, in the closed theatre, music begins again.

fin_spectateur_immobile.title: The Still Spectator
fin_spectateur_immobile.narrator: Sometimes the most dangerous choice is not to do anything.
fin_spectateur_immobile.text:
You sit down.

You're not leaving your seat.

You're not talking.

You're not looking for an explanation.

The play continues.

Then the actors disappear.

The scenery is fading.

The room's getting dark.

It's just you.

And a voice, very close:

**"At last. Someone who agrees to look to the end. *

fin_fausse_sortie.title: The False Exit
fin_fausse_sortie.narrator: We can take the right road and get to the wrong place.
fin_fausse_sortie.text:
You're leaving Valmire on foot.

The road goes down between the black trees.

For a long time, you can't hear the music.

That's almost reassuring.

Then the trees drift away.

In front of you:

The station.

The same yellow lamp.

Same rain.

Same train coming.

In your pocket, the invitation came back.

attendre_train.title: Waiting for Departure
attendre_train.narrator: Denying an invitation is still a way to respond.
attendre_train.text:
You stay on the dock.

Travelers leave one by one.

The music of the city becomes more distant.

An hour passes.

Or maybe just a few minutes.

At the end of the rails, a light is approaching.

choices.text: Change your mind and join the city

rester_cafe.title: Stay at the Café
rester_cafe.narrator: It is easy to confuse heat and safety.
rester_cafe.text:
You stay at the cafe.

The pianist always plays.

Clients stop looking at you.

For a moment, Valmire seems almost welcoming.

The performance can start without you.

choices.text: Get out before it's too late.

traitement_clinique.title: Treatment
traitement_clinique.narrator: An explanation can be a door. Or a trap.
traitement_clinique.text:
In a metal cabinet, you find a box of tablets.

The label is almost erased.

> Dissociative disorders.
> Perceptive repetitions.
> Theatrical obsessions.

A treatment protocol is set aside.

It is signed by a doctor whose name has been scratched.

suivre_acteurs.title: Follow Actors
suivre_acteurs.narrator: Some paths become roles when you follow them too long.
suivre_acteurs.text:
You follow the actors in a narrow corridor.

Nobody's stopping you.

Nobody seems surprised.

An open dressing room awaits you.

Inside, a suit is placed on a chair.

It's exactly your size.

errer_ville.title: Error in Valmire
errer_ville.narrator: Some cities don't cross. They're getting deeper.
errer_ville.text:
You walk without following the music.

You're avoiding the theater.

You walk past closed houses, dusty windows, alleyways where the rain seems to fall slower.

The more you get away from the central square, the more Valmire seems empty.

choices.text: Continue wandering away from the theatre
Search for a road out of town

route_sortie.title: The Exit Road
route_sortie.narrator: The boundaries of a city are rarely the ones on the maps.
route_sortie.text:
You finally find a road that leaves Valmire.

No sign.

No car.

Only trees, rain and black gravel.

Behind you, the music of the theatre becomes almost inaudible.

choices.text: Follow the road to the end

boucle_lucidite.title: Not yet
boucle_lucidite.narrator: You're not ready. Your mind knows before you even put your hand on the handle. Something is missing — a connection, a truth that you haven't entered somewhere in Valmire yet.
boucle_lucidite.text:
The door opens.

The darkness on the other side is not an ordinary darkness. She thinks. She's watching you.

And then — a dizziness. A white one in your memory. A suspended moment.

When you come back to mind, you're in the hallway. The door is closed behind you. Like you never moved.

Valmire's still waiting for you. There are things you haven't really looked at.
